evals.prompt: I'm about to refactor our login form. It currently works fine. How do I capture baseline evidence before I start editing so I can prove the refactor didn't break anything?
evals.expected_output: Before editing: Take a screenshot of the login form (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-form.png). Try a valid login and capture the response JSON (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-success.json). Try invalid credentials and capture the error (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-error.json). Save a summary to e2e-evidence/baseline/assessment.md. Now you can refactor. After refactor: re-run the same 3 captures and compare — if the form still works and API responses are identical, NO REGRESSION.

evals.prompt: I want to update our Express API from v4 to v5. What baseline evidence do I capture before the upgrade, and how do I prove the upgrade didn't break anything?
evals.expected_output: Baseline before upgrade: Run all 6 API validation steps (health check, CRUD cycle, auth, error responses, pagination, rate limiting if applicable). Save every curl response to e2e-evidence/baseline/api-*. After upgrade: re-run the same 6 steps, save to e2e-evidence/api-after-upgrade/*. Compare: status codes, response structure, auth behavior. If all match, no regression. If any differ, investigate.
